Dubai Traffic Intelligence · live citizen monitor

Independent, open-data monitoring of Dubai's road network — 27 corridors · 58 points · 4 data sources · $0 budget

💡 Proposals evidence-based · preview

📰 What the data is saying

Auto-written from the measurements each cycle — today's findings first, then the standing patterns. Filter by theme:

How long does it take to cross each corridor?

Click any road for its deep-dive (profile, incidents, what an accident does, lead/lag, toll). Hover a column heading for what it means. Ranked worst-peak first.
Collecting travel times…

When does traffic build? — congestion by hour × day

Mean level-weighted jam length (km) per cycle. Darker = heavier. UAE weekend (Sat/Sun) rows highlighted.
lighterheavier

Congestion over the study ⓘ weighted jam-km

A single congestion index per cycle: level-weighted jam-km, full timeline.

Incidents by hour of day

Average new accidents & closures per day at each hour (Waze).

Accident & incident hotspots

Live heat = where traffic is jammed right now (last sweep). Bottlenecks = where jams form most over the whole study. Incidents (official) & Waze accidents (crowd) = last-24h reports, coloured by recency.

Official incidents by category

Dubai Police logged incident types (study baseline + live).

Worst peak windows

Heaviest hour×day congestion buckets (Waze).
DayHourWeighted jam-km

Official incidents by hour of day

Average Dubai Police incidents per day at each local hour — the clearest "when do crashes happen" signal.

Official incidents per day

Daily incident count & serious subset. Watch the school-holiday regime change (~6 Jul).

🛡 Safety — accident types, severity & where they happen

Official Dubai Police accidents by type, how dangerous each is (% serious), the hour it peaks, and whether it clusters at a traffic signal (≤50 m) or a roundabout (≤75 m) — road features from OpenStreetMap. The point: volume is not danger — the commonest types are nuisances, the harm concentrates in a few.

📅 Day-of-week demand pattern

Mean extra delay above each corridor's own empirical free-flow, broken down by day of week — purely descriptive, no policy claim. Built to sidestep the collector's disputed "weekend" flag (Sat/Sun) rather than resolve it.

🌫 Weather sensitivity — does delay move with weather?

Simple correlation (r) between each corridor's delay and dust (PM10) / temperature / wind / visibility. Descriptive only — not controlled for time-of-day, so a moderate r may partly reflect a confound (e.g. hot afternoons overlapping the PM peak) rather than a direct physical effect.

✈️ DXB airport arrivals airport demand

Average flight arrivals per day at each local hour at Dubai International — the airport-demand wave that feeds taxi/pickup and airport-road surges ~20–40 min after each arrival bank. A new no-auth open-data feed (data.dubai dataset 463958, ~30-min refresh — same pipeline as the police feed).

Corridor speed performance (TomTom)

Speed ratio = current / free-flow (lower = worse). TTI = travel-time index. PTI = planning-time (reliability). Ranked worst peak first.

Weather vs congestion

Each point is a cycle: dust (PM10) vs level-weighted jam-km.

Event calendar

Holidays, school dates, fuel changes & events overlaid on the study window.
DateEventExpected effect

Salik tolls & route choice does pricing move traffic?

Dubai's variable tolls (peak AED 6 · off-peak AED 4 · free 1–6am · flat AED 4 Sundays). When the toll bites, do drivers crowd onto the free parallel roads? Each row compares tolled vs non-toll routes that go to ~the same place.

Rat-run probes experiment

Audited 2026-06-13: rebuilt as same-origin/destination A/B pairs to measure rat-running directly. A pair "confirms" only if the bypass beats the arterial during arterial peaks. First-pass scan found mostly closures-coded-as-jams (see 🚧 map layer), so these probes are the real test. Data accumulates from today.

City-wide screen recurrence x timing x bypass

Every minor-street jam cluster in the city, scored against its nearest arterial bottleneck (method in the vault: 3-component product). Honesty rule: congestion observed is not through-traffic proven - candidates for probes, never conclusions.

The cost of congestion estimate

What peak-hour delay costs the average commuter on these corridors per year — time, fuel, and CO₂. Method: delay-hours × UAE value-of-time (AED /hr ≈ half the average wage), speed-based emission factors, idle-fuel burn at AED /L. Per-commuter (2 peak trips × 230 work-days), not a city total.

Corridor reliability PTI

How much buffer you must leave to arrive on time. PTI ranks the least-predictable corridors — RTA's reliability lens.
CorridorPTIBufferFree→p95

Best time to travel

Per corridor, when to leave to dodge the worst — and the minutes you save vs the peak.
CorridorAM bestPM bestSave

Benchmark vs the official indices TomTom 2025 / RTA

Our independent measurements next to the published figures — the credibility check. Close agreement validates the method.
